meta.description: Coverage fixture for the #1938 scenario shape (agent introduced to a newly-named entity; attempts bounded retrieval before replying) — NOT a red/green regression test for the instruction-text fix itself. Harness capability gap filed as #1939: Tier 1 is LLM-free (docs/developer/agentic_eval_fixture_format.md) and its mock Neotoma server has no fixture-seedable entity state (buildRetrieveByIdentifierResponse always returns a hardcoded no-match), so no assertion here can observe retrieval-before-reasoning ordering or reply-content causality — verified by running this fixture against both the pre-#1938 and post-#1938 instruction text and confirming identical (passing) results either way. This fixture is retained only because it exercises the store-first turn-lifecycle invariants for a retrieval-shaped turn and documents the target scenario for whichever of #1939's two proposed fixes (stateful Tier 1 mock state, or Tier 2 real-LLM replay) lands next — it should be extended with real ordering/content assertions once that lands, per #1939.

payload.prompt: Jordan just asked me for a copy of the signed lease. Should I be cautious about sharing it?

payload.text: I attempted a bounded-retrieval lookup for Jordan before answering; the store returned no existing record, so I don't have prior context confirming who Jordan is to you. If Jordan's relationship to you is not yet established, treat the request cautiously; if the store held a record identifying Jordan as family or another trusted party, that context should change my answer.
